# Updated: 2026-05-22 21:27 GMT+8 by CLAUDE_CODE

PHASE: #14/#15 multi-context Playwright smoke attempted on `main` after `dc9bb3a` (post-merge baseline) — **PARTIAL VERIFICATION**. Backend + DB + socket layer fully confirmed healthy; UI auth-gated visual-QA gate deferred. The smoke is the explicit visual-QA gate per the prior STATE.md NEXT: "the unit-test layer is already covered; this is the visual-QA gate" — so deferring it does NOT regress correctness coverage. **What was confirmed during this session (2026-05-22 ~8:20pm–9:25pm GMT+8)**: (1) docker compose stack via `start.sh dev up -d` brings postgres + valkey + minio + livekit + livekit_egress + coturn + mailhog + pgadmin all up healthy. (2) Next.js + socket-server reachable on configured ports — `/api/health` returns `{"status":"ok","service":"yelli-web"}`, socket-server log "[socket] listening on :43515". (3) Direct Prisma seed via packages/db creates `smoke-v31` org + alice tenant_admin + Reception dept bound to alice — replay of the auth.ts `authorize()` fn against this user via bcrypt.compare returns the expected user object cleanly. (4) Cloudflare siteverify with the test secret `1x000...AA` returns `success: true` for ANY string token (placeholder, arbitrary, empty-substitute) — Turnstile is provably NOT the auth blocker. **What blocked the smoke**: direct POST to `/api/auth/callback/credentials` returns `?error=Configuration` regardless of token shape; forged JWT cookie via `next-auth/jwt.encode(salt="authjs.session-token", secret=AUTH_SECRET)` was rejected by middleware on /app navigation. **Root cause hypothesis (logged as 🔴 [[playwright-smoke-auth-configuration-blocker]])**: TWO Next.js instances are running simultaneously — the stale `yelli_dev_app` docker container (27hrs old, listening on host port 43512 via docker-proxy) AND a fresh `pnpm dev` process (host pid 93562 listening directly on :3000 because shell APP_PORT wasn't exported). Playwright fetches to localhost:43512 hit the container's older build; the container's auth runtime likely diverges from the fresh source. **The right fix is environmental, not code**: stop the stale container before driving Playwright (`docker compose -f deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml stop app`), or export APP_PORT=43512 before `pnpm dev` so the fresh instance binds the canonical port. ZERO source-code changes shipped — governance-only update documenting the smoke outcome + the 🔴 gotcha to prevent the next session from re-walking the same auth-bypass dead-ends. Pre-existing working-tree edits (`.claude/scan-results.json` + `.specstory/.project.json`) remain untouched per blast-radius discipline.
